channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Strong earthquake in Taiwan injures 27 and causes scattered damage
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — A 6.4-magnitude earthquake struck southern Taiwan early Tuesday, leaving 27 people with minor injuries and some reported damage. The quake hit at 12:17 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump pardons nearly all Jan. 6 rioters on first day of presidency
item.description: President Trump pardoned nearly all Jan. 6 defendants on Monday, making good on a long-standing campaign promise just hours into his second presidency.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Authorities say a US border patrol agent was fatally shot in Vermont south of border
item.description: COVENTRY, Vt. (AP) — A U.S. Border Patrol agent was fatally shot Monday on a highway in northern Vermont south of the Canadian border, authorities said. The death was confirmed by the FBI and Benjamine Huffman, acting secretary of the Department of Homeland Security in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fire crews in Southern California quickly extinguish brush fires amid extreme fire weather
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Firefighters quickly extinguished several brush fires that erupted Monday in Southern California amid windy and dry conditions.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump issues sweeping pardon of 1,500 Jan. 6 defendants, including rioters who attacked police
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Monday pardoned or commuted the prison sentences of all of the 1,500-plus people charged with crimes in the Jan. 6, 2021, U.S.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Bitter cold spreads across much of the US as Texas and the South brace for rare winter storm
item.description: Frigid temperatures engulfed the South on Monday ahead of a winter storm that's expected to spread heavy snow and disruptive ice around a region from Texas to north Florida that rarely sees such weather, sending residents rushing to insulate pipes, check heating systems and stock up on emergency sup
item.dc:creator: SARAH BRUMFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate passes Laken Riley Act in first move after Trump inauguration
item.description: The Senate on Monday passed the Laken Riley Act, making the immigration-related bill the first piece of legislation to make it through the upper chamber in the new Congress and putting it a step closer to being signed into law by President Trump. Senators voted 64-35 on the bill.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Former Planned Parenthood president, women's rights activist Cecile Richards has died at 67
item.description: CONCORD, N.H. (AP) — Cecile Richards, a national leader for abortion access and women’s rights who led Planned Parenthood for 12 tumultuous years, has died. She was 67. Richards died Monday at home in New York “surrounded by family and her ever-loyal dog, Ollie,” her family said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY RAMER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Here's who skipped Trump's inauguration
item.description: President Trump was sworn into office for a second time on Monday before an audience in the Capitol that included his predecessor, former President Biden, as well as former Presidents Obama, Clinton and George W. Bush and multiple current and former officials and lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: WATCH: Carrie Underwood sings 'America the Beautiful' a cappella after apparent tech issue at inauguration
item.description: An apparent technical issue didn't stop Carrie Underwood from delivering "America the Beautiful" during the second inauguration of President Trump on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump administration sued over DOGE as soon as swearing-in over
item.description: President Trump’s new "Department of Government Efficiency" (DOGE) was sued multiple times before his inauguration ceremony in the Capitol Rotunda concluded. In the moments after Trump’s swearing-in, three lawsuits were filed in D.C.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WFLA, NBC

item.title: WATCH: Donald Trump, JD Vance sworn into office
item.description: Donald Trump was sworn in as the 47th president Monday, promising a “revolution of common sense” and taking charge as Republicans assume unified control of Washington and set out to reshape the country’s institutions.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, CHRIS MEGERIAN and MICHELLE L. PRICE Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Costco workers authorize nationwide strike
item.description: Thousands of Costco workers nationwide voted Sunday to authorize a strike if a contract is not reached by the end of the month.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KTLA 5

item.title: Longtime Planned Parenthood head Cecile Richards dies at 67
item.description: Cecile Richards, the former president of Planned Parenthood, died at the age of 67 on Monday after a battle with cancer.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, KRON4

item.title: At Trump's second inaugural, a changed Washington and a full embrace from Republicans
item.description: It was a changed Washington that greeted Donald Trump when he took the oath of office as president a second time.
item.dc:creator: Mary Clare Jalonick, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: WATCH LIVE: Trump signs executive orders, celebrates inauguration
item.description: On a frigid holiday Monday in Washington, D.C., Donald Trump took the oath of office for a second time to become the 47th president of the United States.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: Trump to promise 'new era of national success' in inaugural address
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is set to promise a “new era of national success” in his inaugural address, according to The Wall Street Journal.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden pardons Fauci, Jan. 6 committee ahead of Trump inauguration
item.description: (The Hill) -- President Joe Biden granted pardons to several prominent public servants Monday who have faced attacks from President-elect Donald Trump in one of his final acts in office. Biden issued pardons for Dr.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Do US adults support Trump's Day 1 actions? Here's what polling shows
item.description: President Donald Trump is beginning to fulfill pledges to crack down on immigration and restore “energy dominance.”.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Thomson-Deveaux And Linley Sanders, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump returning to power after unprecedented comeback, emboldened to reshape American institutions
item.description: Donald Trump, who overcame impeachments, criminal indictments and a pair of assassination attempts to win another term in the White House, will be sworn in as the 47th president on Monday, taking charge as Republicans assume unified control of Washington and set out to reshape the country's institut
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, CHRIS MEGERIAN and MICHELLE PRICE Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump inauguration: Mom of jogger allegedly killed by migrant praises friendship with incoming president
item.description: Patty Morin's quest for justice against migrant crime led to stiffer immigration laws and a trip to President-elect Donald Trump's inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How TikTok grew from a fun app for teens into a potential national security threat
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — If it feels like TikTok has been around forever, that's probably because it has, at least if you're measuring via internet time.
item.dc:creator: DAVID HAMILTON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How TikTok grew from a fun app for teens into a potential national security threat
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — If it feels like TikTok has been around forever, that's probably because it has, at least if you're measuring via internet time.
item.dc:creator: DAVID HAMILTON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Heavy snow and frigid Arctic blast put 70 million across the US under winter storm warnings
item.description: Tens of millions of residents along the East Coast are bracing for several inches of snow Sunday followed by dangerously cold temperatures that will take hold in much of the country from the Northern Plains to the tip of Maine.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bird flu found in a Georgia commercial flock for the 1st time amid the nationwide outbreak
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — For the first time since the 2022 countrywide outbreak, bird flu hit a poultry producer in Georgia, the nation's top state for chicken production.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Can you spot black ice? What to do if you start sliding or skidding
item.description: Black ice forms without creating bubbles, meaning it blends in with the road and becomes hard to detect.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Whitley, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump returns to Washington for celebrations ahead of inauguration
item.description: Donald Trump returned to Washington Saturday evening to celebrate with family, supporters and political allies ahead of his second inauguration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: TikTok says it's in the process of restoring service to US users
item.description: Millions of TikTok users in the United States were no longer able to watch or post videos on the social media platform as a federal ban on the immensely popular video-sharing app took effect Sunday amid questions over how long the blackout might last.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: These lip balm ingredients may be making your chapped lips worse, experts say
item.description: It's tempting to reach for lip balm during the dry, winter months, but it may not be helping you at all.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: Mahomes and Kelce help Chiefs to a 23-14 win over Texans and another AFC title game trip
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — Patrick Mahomes has never lost in the divisional round of the playoffs with the Kansas City Chiefs. The Houston Texans still have never won.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: California officials warn against price gouging as rents soar in fire-stricken Los Angeles
item.description: Southern California's expensive housing market is going to get a lot more competitive after deadly firestorms torched more than 12,000 homes and other structures in the Los Angeles area, leaving tens of thousands of people without a place to stay.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Most voters in new survey seek toned-down Trump agenda
item.description: Over 60% of Americans polled disagree with Trump's idea to replace thousands of career civil-service workers.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WGN 9

item.title: Pepsi discontinues 'permanent' flavor, debuts a completely different one
item.description: Pepsi is planning to debut a brand-new flavor this month, marking its first “permanent” addition to the brand’s beverage portfolio in four years.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WGN 9
